$comment: Net-class DRC rulebook. Templated from a 4-layer high-current board. SPECIFIC = net names, widths, currents, keepout coords. GENERAL = the class taxonomy, the global-rule keys, the max_width>=20 gotcha, the apply_sequence discipline. Assert 0 nets on 'default' after applying (a class keyed on a stale net name silently matches nothing and PASSES DRC — fail-silent).
board: <W×H mm>, <N> layers, <stackup, e.g. F 1oz / In1 GND 0.5oz / In2 VSYS 0.5oz / B 1oz>
replaces_live_ruleset: <name of the wrong ruleset NOT to edit in place>

net_classes.name: POWER_MOTOR
net_classes.note: neck to 0.8 only at SOT-23 pad

diff_pairs.name: USB
diff_pairs.note: FS speed → electrically short; match is best-practice, 0-vias+solid-ref is the hard rule

geometry_keepouts_MANUAL.why: 0.5mm-pitch mask web < 0.15mm bridges; rules can't fix geometry — keepout at the connector mouth

cautions: power-carrying pours (GND_PLANE/POWER_HC/POWER) MUST have max_width_mm >= 20 or the max-track check false-fails on board-spanning pours
assert 0 nets on 'default' after apply — a class keyed on a renamed net silently matches nothing
DRC gate = this board's OWN tool with THIS ruleset; never cross tools
apply_sequence: clone the wrong live ruleset, don't edit it in place
create the 10 classes, assign every net
set inner planes UNCHECKED so the autorouter can't carve them
run DRC to confirm the ruleset loads clean on the empty board
